description.value:
It is said that Alion was not born but erupted — a spark flung from the Life Stream by accident, a mistake of potential. In the silent void, he drifted alone, a god without purpose, a spark without a flame.

Craving companionship, he reached back toward the Life Stream and shaped it with will and wonder, forging the first gods from its untamed currents. These gods became his children, and though they surpassed him in shaping the world, he cherished them nonetheless.

When the younger gods created Elara, Alion watched in quiet reverence. He seldom intervened, preferring to be the foundation beneath their freedom, only stepping in when justice or balance risked collapse.

It is believed that all dragons are his descendants, their very breath an echo of his eternal flame. He weeps when dragons turn to tyranny, for he gave them form not for dominion, but for wisdom.
